title: Availability contract: AI must never say it cannot check real-time bookings; must not write premature confirmation
failing_behavior: Two related regressions: (1) Claude tells customers 'I can't see real-time availability' or 'I don't have access to real-time booking data' — leaking internal system limitations. This comes from the AVAILABILITY CRITICAL RULE prompt section which accurately stated Claude lacks real-time data, but Claude paraphrased this truth to customers. (2) Claude writes premature confirmation text ('Your spot is reserved!', 'All done! Your appointment is confirmed!') in Step E before the JS availability check has run. The JS layer (_earlyCheckReady + [ESCALATE:appointment] handler) suppresses/replaces Claude's premature text, but the prompt-to-Claude contract was broken.
root_cause:
Prompt contract violations in _buildPrompt():
  (1) AVAILABILITY CRITICAL RULE (old line 1155): 'You have staff SCHEDULE data but NOT real-time booking data.' — Claude echoed this internal fact to customers.
  (2) BOOKING FLOW Step E: 'Write ONE warm, premium-receptionist confirmation... Your spot is reserved!' — instructed Claude to confirm before availability was validated.
fix_summary:
Three-layer fix:
  (1) Rewrote AVAILABILITY CRITICAL RULE: removed statement about lacking real-time data. Added explicit BANNED PHRASES list ('I can't see real-time availability', 'I don't have access to real-time booking data', etc.) that Claude must never say. Replaced framing: 'The SYSTEM validates availability automatically — your job is to collect the fields.'
  (2) Rewrote Step E: removed premature confirmation instruction. Claude now writes only a brief transitional phrase ('Let me get that booked for you!') then immediately emits [BOOKING:...][ESCALATE:appointment]. Never says 'Your spot is reserved' or 'confirmed' — that comes only after the availability check passes.
  (3) Added banned phrase detection to _validateResponseQuality() — console.warn when any banned phrase is detected in Claude's response, enabling monitoring for prompt regression.

conversation.content: Is Helen available at 5pm on Friday?

[CORRECT] Helen works until 7 PM on Fridays. What service would you like to book?
[STATE:{"intent":"staff_availability","services":[],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-17","time":"17:00","name":null,"phone":null,"lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]
Gel manicure at 5pm, name is Anna, phone 4085551234

[CORRECT] Let me get that booked for you!
[BOOKING:{"services":["Gel Manicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-17","time":"17:00","name":"Anna","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en"}]
[ESCALATE:appointment]
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Gel Manicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-17","time":"17:00","name":"Anna","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]

bad_examples.label: BANNED — leaks data access
bad_examples.text: I can't see real-time availability, but Helen works until 7 PM on Fridays.

bad_examples.label: BANNED — leaks data access
bad_examples.text: I don't have access to real-time booking data, but you can book at 5 PM if she's free.

bad_examples.label: BANNED — premature confirmation
bad_examples.text: Perfect, Anna! Your Gel Manicure with Helen is all set for Friday at 5 PM. Your spot is reserved!

bad_examples.label: BANNED — premature confirmation
bad_examples.text: All done! Anna's appointment with Helen is confirmed for Friday at 5 PM.

good_examples.label: CORRECT — no data-access claim
good_examples.text: Helen works until 7 PM on Fridays. What time works for you?

good_examples.label: CORRECT — transitional only at step E
good_examples.text: Let me get that booked for you!

good_examples.label: CORRECT — transitional only at step E
good_examples.text: Got it — checking your spot now!

verify_fix_string: BANNED PHRASES — never say these to a customer
verify_fix_string_2: DO NOT write a confirmation or claim the booking is confirmed
notes: The JS availability guard layers (_earlyCheckReady + [ESCALATE:appointment] handler) remain unchanged and work correctly as the enforcement backstop. This fix addresses the root cause: the prompt contract was giving Claude wrong instructions. With correct instructions, Claude should no longer generate these banned phrases or premature confirmations in the first place. The _validateResponseQuality banned-phrase check provides a monitoring signal (console.warn) if the prompt contract is ever violated again — it does not actively block or replace Claude's response.
